metric_value: Most popular TP monitor worldwide
notes: Aberdeen states CICS is 'by far the most popular TP monitor'; IMS described as 'today's most-used DBMS' for mainframes

metric_value: Unix, OS/2, Windows NT, Digital VAX, MVS/ESA servers; Unix/DOS/OS/2/Win95/NT clients
notes: Store-and-forward messaging with broad platform coverage; supports TCP/IP, SNA, Netbios, DECnet simultaneously

metric_value: Performance approaching RPC
notes: Aberdeen finds MQSeries can deliver performance approaching that of remote procedure calls

metric_value: Most popular LAN groupware product
notes: Described as 'today's most popular product in the LAN groupware market'

metric_value: Most-used mainframe DBMS
notes: IMS described as 'perhaps today's most-used DBMS' for mainframe environments

metric_value: Very-large-database data-warehousing proven
notes: DB2 Parallel Edition proven in very-large-database data-warehousing situations

metric_value: Web servers creating scalability bottlenecks
notes: New web architectures adding browsers/web servers 'often creating scalability bottlenecks' — driving need for TP monitors

metric_value: Migration path for IBM mainframe shops to network computing
notes: Positioned as 'relatively risk-free and less costly migration path' for IBM customers moving to network computing

metric_value: Will add third-party and IBM tools for ORBs and commercial-messaging applications
notes: Aberdeen anticipates MQSeries will add tools supporting Object Request Brokers and specific commercial-messaging applications

metric_value: Renamed and expanded; ORB integrations added
notes: MQSeries renamed WebSphere MQ in 2002; IBM MQ in 2014. CORBA/ORB integrations were added. Prediction confirmed.

metric_value: Will continue integrating products and development tools with the Internet
notes: Aberdeen anticipates Lotus Notes will continue to integrate with the Internet

metric_value: Sold by IBM to HCL for $1.8B; lost collaboration market to Microsoft 365
notes: Notes/Domino never successfully transitioned to cloud-first model. IBM sold to HCL Technologies in 2019. Microsoft Exchange/O365 dominates.

metric_value: Will extend multimedia capabilities for Web-site and Internet needs
notes: Aberdeen anticipates Database Server will extend multimedia capabilities for Web/Internet

metric_value: DB2 UDB shipped with extensive multimedia/XML/Extender support
notes: DB2 Universal Database (UDB) expanded Extenders; later added XML/JSON support. Prediction confirmed.

metric_name: TP monitor as Internet scalability layer
metric_value: TP-monitor middleware most critical to Internet architecture scalability
notes: Aberdeen cites own viewpoint: TP-monitor-type middleware is 'most critical to scalability' for Internet architectures

metric_value: Java application servers (WebSphere, JBoss, WebLogic) confirmed TP-monitor pattern at Internet scale
notes: IBM WebSphere Application Server (1998+) embodied the TP-monitor-as-middleware prediction. Entire Java EE ecosystem validated this architecture.

metric_value: Encina removed from TXSeries V6.1 in 2006
notes: Encina's DCE dependency made it legacy; IBM removed Encina from TXSeries in 2006. CICS prevailed as the durable TP monitor.

metric_value: CICS TS 6.3 released September 2025; actively developed with Jakarta EE 10, Spring Boot 3 support
notes: CICS proved its 57-year durability. CICS for IBM i sunsetted 2024; z/OS version thriving.

metric_value: Assured once-and-only-once delivery with connection independence
notes: Key differentiator: assured delivery regardless of connection state; supports unit-of-work commitment for groups of messages

metric_value: CICS Gateway for Java; Web and Lotus Notes integration
notes: Transaction Server includes CICS Gateway for Java; DCE-compliant; supports DE-Light for handheld/remote devices

metric_value: Large data types up to 2 GB; Relational Extenders for complex types
notes: DB2 supports objects up to 2 GB; character/double-byte character types for internationalization; Universal-Server bundles

metric_value: Covers both IBM and non-IBM sites via open middleware interoperability
notes: ETS products position IBM to capture both existing IBM mainframe customers and new network-computing deployments

metric_value: IMS accessible via Web browser through Internet Connection Server / MQSeries gateway
notes: MQSeries Internet Gateway (Internet Connection Server) allows Web browser to connect to IMS or DB2 databases

metric_value: ETS deserves IS buyers' closest attention; highly attractive network-computing family
notes: Aberdeen: 'If there is one IBM network-computing product family that deserves IS buyers' closest attention, this is the one'

metric_value: Internet/Intranet adding new TP patterns for e-commerce
notes: New transaction patterns for e-commerce (extra-enterprise customers) driving need for extended TP infrastructure
